title: Setaria basiclada
taxonomy: Setaria basiclada is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Setaria, which contains several species of grasses. The genus Setaria is further classified under the subfamily Panicoideae and the order Poales. Setaria basiclada is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. It is a type of annual grass that grows in a variety of environments.

In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
